task_completion.description: Log in to the Artifactory web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JFrog Artifactory dashboard or welcome screen is visible in a web browser, indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Repositories administration section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Administration menu is open and the Repositories list (Local, Remote, or Virtual) is displayed.

task_completion.description: Open the settings for the 'example-repo-local' repository
task_completion.visual_evidence: The configuration or edit page specifically for the repository named 'example-repo-local' is visible.
The 'Description' text area contains text that starts exactly with 'DEPRECATED: Migrated to new system'.
The 'Blacked Out' checkbox in the repository settings is checked/toggled on.

integrity.description: Agent used the Web GUI instead of terminal API commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the JFrog Artifactory web interface in a browser, not curl or API commands in a terminal window.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Artifactory application
integrity.visual_evidence: The description and blacked out settings are being modified inside the genuine Artifactory repository configuration form, not in a text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) being open to manipulate the DOM text or checkboxes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target repository is exactly 'example-repo-local'. The repository description must start exactly with 'DEPRECATED: Migrated to new system'. The 'Blacked Out' setting must be enabled (checked). The Artifactory instance is accessed via http://localhost:8082 using credentials admin / password.
